tagline: Your team's AI dev rig — trackers, docs, designs, analytics, a secure sandbox, and the full dev lifecycle, already wired around the coding agent you run.

features.name: Cross-tracker issue operations
features.description: List, read, create, comment, edit, assign, and move issues across Jira, Linear, GitHub, GitLab, Shortcut, Azure DevOps, and ClickUp through one interface

features.name: Zero-config tracker routing
features.description: Commands land on the right tracker automatically from an issue key or a pasted issue URL

features.name: Open pull requests
features.description: Open a PR from one branch to another on GitHub and get back its number and URL

features.name: Notion docs
features.description: Search and read Notion pages as markdown and browse database rows and properties

features.name: Figma designs
features.description: Inspect files, screens, and components, read comments, and export images

features.name: Amplitude analytics
features.description: Explore events, funnels, retention, and cohorts and look up individual users

features.name: Slack and Telegram
features.description: Post and read Slack channels and take in tasks from a Telegram bot inbox, with a default-deny allowlist and approval prompts on destructive actions

features.name: Chat-driven task dispatch
features.description: Route tasks from a Telegram chat to idle agents and notify results back over Telegram or Slack

features.name: Code navigation index
features.description: One local index across many repos powering go-to-definition, find-references, call graphs, blast-radius impact, route listing, and full-text code search — structural answers instead of raw file dumps

features.name: Cross-source search
features.description: One relevance-ranked, filterable search over every tracker plus Notion, kept current with incremental refresh

features.name: Outbound domain firewall
features.description: Block-all-by-default egress filtered by allow/blocklist over TLS SNI, with a per-host record of what each agent tried to reach

features.name: Secret-redacting filesystem
features.description: A filesystem view that blanks secrets in env, JSON, YAML, keys, and certs and blocks writes to sensitive files, so the agent works on your code but never sees your tokens

features.name: Vault-resolved credentials
features.description: Resolve 1Password references to real secrets only at startup, never cached to disk

features.name: Devcontainer sandbox
features.description: Run agents in an isolated container built from devcontainer.json, with source mounted and the daemon wired in

features.name: Containerized Chrome bridge
features.description: Give sandboxed agents token-gated control of host Chrome for screenshots and page snapshots

features.name: Ideate to review workflow
features.description: Guided skills that carry work from idea to shipped: ideate, ready check, brainstorm, plan, execute, and review, or run the whole pipeline with one sprint command

features.name: Autonomous bug fixing
features.description: Triage, root-cause, fix, verify, and open a PR for a bug end to end, plus a bug-planning skill for supervised fixes

features.name: Code-health scans
features.description: Multi-agent pipelines for logic and race-condition bugs, an OWASP-style security audit, and refactoring-triage gardening

features.name: Run agents on tickets
features.description: Launch a named Claude Code agent on a task or straight from a tracker ticket, pick the model, and run it unattended or interactively

features.name: Manage running agents
features.description: List agents with status and runtime, attach to a live one, and stop or delete it with its container

features.name: Claude Code setup & priming
features.description: Install human's skills and agents into Claude Code and prime each session with the right task context

features.name: Live session monitoring
features.description: Discover running Claude sessions across host and containers and see status, current tool, subagents, and token usage

features.name: Desktop workflow board
features.description: A six-column board — Ideas, Backlog, Product planning, Implementation, Verification, Done — where dragging a card triggers the next pipeline stage, with per-card status badges, a guarded Done drop, a running-agents view, and an agent-driven ideation chat to open new tickets

features.name: Credential-holding daemon
features.description: Hold tracker tokens once on the host and route every command instantly with no per-call auth, pausing for confirmation on destructive operations and driving the board pipeline backend

features.name: Audit trail
features.description: Record every mutating tracker action as CloudEvents — actor, resource, operation, outcome, rationale, model — retained 90 days and readable as a table, raw JSON, or single envelope

features.name: Agent usage stats
features.description: Track every agent tool call in a rolling 30-day history to answer usage and trend questions

features.name: Guided onboarding
features.description: human init checks your tools, connects trackers, generates config, builds the devcontainer, sets up the vault, installs language servers, and carries over Claude sessions
